title: Daemon Operations, Upgrades, Recovery, Cleanup, Orchestration Boundary, and Capstone De… · Delivery Case: Docker Daemon Config Live Restore
summary: An operations board must approve a production container system through daemon upgrade, disk pressure, backup recovery, credential rotation, and migration pressure. Planned artifact: Add and verify the docker daemon config live restore increment in production Docker operations and release defense. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Validate daemon configuration, data root, logging, DNS, proxies, registries, authorization, metrics, reload boundaries, live restore, and rollback.
Gate tests, builds, policies, multi-platform manifests, signatures, attestations, promotion, deployment digest, rollback, and post-release verification in CI.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example board reviews a digest-pinned multi-service release with an untested rollback, stale backup, daemon upgrade, capacity uncertainty, and unclear ownership. Defend rollout, recovery, compatibility, and residual limits. Competency probe: Validate daemon configuration, data root, logging, DNS, proxies, registries, authorization, metrics, reload boundaries, live restore, and rollback. A defensible solution applies Docker Daemon Config Live Restore, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to production Docker operations and release defense.

content.code:
# Worked focus: docker-daemon-config-live-restore — Validate daemon configuration, data root, logging, DNS, proxies, registries, authorization, metrics, reload boundaries, live restore, and rollback.
  # Evidence: docker-worked-docker-production-operations-defense-5
  - competency: docker-worked-docker-production-operations-defense-5
    artifact: production-defense.yaml
    decision: worked docker production operations defense 5
    scenario-variant: 4
    environment: ephemeral development host with constrained disk and memory
    fault-injection: rebuild after the approved base digest is withdrawn
    retained-proof: accessible recovery runbook with rollback rehearsal timing
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      release: digest-pinned
      rollout: health-and-observation-gated
      rollback: rehearsed-previous-digest
      backup-restore: timed-and-proven
      upgrade: compatibility-canary
      capacity: measured-limits
      handoff: accessible-runbook
    verify: docker compose config --quiet && docker buildx imagetools inspect candidate@sha256:release
    failure: Reject latest-tag rollout, untested rollback, backup without restore, blind daemon upgrade, or a container boundary claimed as orchestrator durability.
    repair: Defend the operating model with immutable identity, canary signals, failure drills, recovery objectives, capacity evidence, ownership, and residual risks.
content.caption: Runnable Docker artifact and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
